tools.name: api
tools.description: Execute a GitHub API call using gh api. Supports all HTTP methods (GET, POST, PATCH, PUT, DELETE). Use this as an escape hatch when specific write tools don't cover your use case.

endpoint.description: GitHub API endpoint, relative to https://api.github.com/ (e.g. 'repos/owner/repo/pulls').

fields.description: jq expression for --jq flag on the gh api call.

jq_filter.description: jq expression to filter/transform the output.

max_lines.description: Return only the first N lines of output.

tail_lines.description: Return only the last N lines of output.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails.

tools.name: pr_create
tools.description: Create a new pull request. Opens a PR from the current or specified branch. Use 'draft' to create a draft PR that isn't ready for review.

body.description: Body text (description) of the pull request.

base.description: Base branch to merge into (e.g. 'main'). Defaults to the repository's default branch.

head.description: Head branch containing the changes (e.g. 'feature/my-feature'). Defaults to the current branch.

draft.description: Create as a draft pull request (not ready for review).

labels.description: Labels to apply to the pull request.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the pull request.

reviewers.description: GitHub usernames to request reviews from.

milestone.description: Milestone name or number to associate with the pull request.

repo.description: GitHub repository in 'owner/repo' format. Defaults to the repo configured in .mcp-gh-tooling.json.

suppress_errors.description: Discard stderr output. When true, gh errors are silenced.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails (non-zero exit).

tools.name: pr_edit
tools.description: Edit an existing pull request's metadata: title, body, base branch, labels, assignees, or milestone. Labels and assignees are added (not replaced). Use the GitHub API tool to remove labels or assignees.

title.description: New title for the pull request.

body.description: New body text (description) for the pull request.

base.description: New base branch to merge into.

labels.description: Labels to add to the pull request.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to add as assignees.

milestone.description: Milestone name or number to associate with the pull request.

repo.description: GitHub repository in 'owner/repo' format. Defaults to the repo configured in .mcp-gh-tooling.json.

suppress_errors.description: Discard stderr output. When true, gh errors are silenced.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails (non-zero exit).

tools.name: pr_ready
tools.description: Mark a draft pull request as ready for review. Transitions the PR from draft state to open/reviewable state.

repo.description: GitHub repository in 'owner/repo' format. Defaults to the repo configured in .mcp-gh-tooling.json.

suppress_errors.description: Discard stderr output. When true, gh errors are silenced.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails (non-zero exit).

tools.name: pr_merge
tools.description: Merge a pull request immediately. Supports merge commit, squash, and rebase strategies. Use 'admin' to bypass branch protection rules when you have admin privileges.

delete_branch.description: Delete the head branch after merging.

admin.description: Use administrator privileges to bypass branch protection rules.

subject.description: Subject line for the merge commit (used with merge and squash methods).

body.description: Body text for the merge commit message.

repo.description: GitHub repository in 'owner/repo' format. Defaults to the repo configured in .mcp-gh-tooling.json.

suppress_errors.description: Discard stderr output. When true, gh errors are silenced.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails (non-zero exit).

tools.name: pr_close
tools.description: Close a pull request without merging. Optionally posts a comment explaining why the PR is being closed.

comment.description: Comment to post when closing the pull request (e.g. explaining the reason).

repo.description: GitHub repository in 'owner/repo' format. Defaults to the repo configured in .mcp-gh-tooling.json.

suppress_errors.description: Discard stderr output. When true, gh errors are silenced.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails (non-zero exit).

tools.name: pr_reopen
tools.description: Reopen a previously closed pull request.

repo.description: GitHub repository in 'owner/repo' format. Defaults to the repo configured in .mcp-gh-tooling.json.

suppress_errors.description: Discard stderr output. When true, gh errors are silenced.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails (non-zero exit).

tools.name: issue_create
tools.description: Create a new GitHub issue. Supports labels, assignees, milestone, and project.

labels.description: Labels to apply to the issue.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue.

milestone.description: Milestone name or number to associate with the issue.

project.description: Project name or URL to add the issue to.

repo.description: GitHub repository in 'owner/repo' format. Defaults to the repo configured in .mcp-gh-tooling.json.

suppress_errors.description: Discard stderr output. When true, gh errors are silenced.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails (non-zero exit).

tools.name: issue_edit
tools.description: Edit an existing issue's metadata: title, body, labels, assignees, or milestone. Labels and assignees are added (not replaced). Use the GitHub API tool to remove labels or assignees.

body.description: New body text (description) for the issue.

labels.description: Labels to add to the issue.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to add as assignees.

milestone.description: Milestone name or number to associate with the issue.

repo.description: GitHub repository in 'owner/repo' format. Defaults to the repo configured in .mcp-gh-tooling.json.

suppress_errors.description: Discard stderr output. When true, gh errors are silenced.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails (non-zero exit).

tools.name: issue_close
tools.description: Close an issue. Optionally specify a reason (completed or not_planned) and post a comment when closing.

comment.description: Comment to post when closing the issue.

repo.description: GitHub repository in 'owner/repo' format. Defaults to the repo configured in .mcp-gh-tooling.json.

suppress_errors.description: Discard stderr output. When true, gh errors are silenced.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails (non-zero exit).

tools.name: issue_reopen
tools.description: Reopen a previously closed issue.

repo.description: GitHub repository in 'owner/repo' format. Defaults to the repo configured in .mcp-gh-tooling.json.

suppress_errors.description: Discard stderr output. When true, gh errors are silenced.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails (non-zero exit).

tools.name: issue_comment
tools.description: Post a comment on an issue.

body.description: Comment text to post on the issue.

repo.description: GitHub repository in 'owner/repo' format. Defaults to the repo configured in .mcp-gh-tooling.json.

suppress_errors.description: Discard stderr output. When true, gh errors are silenced.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails (non-zero exit).

tools.name: pr_review_submit
tools.description: Submit a review on a pull request, optionally batching inline code comments in a single call. This mirrors the web UI flow: start a review, attach line comments, and submit with an event (approve / request changes / comment). When 'comments' is omitted or empty, this acts as a plain event-only review submission. When 'comments' is provided, the commit_id is auto-fetched from the PR's head if not passed explicitly. Use ```suggestion blocks inside a comment body for one-click suggested changes.

body.description: Overall review body text (the top-level review summary). Required for request_changes.

comments.description: Inline review comments to attach to this review. Each item targets a file and line in the PR diff. Optional — omit for a plain event-only review.

path.description: File path relative to repo root.

line.description: Line number in the file (in the PR's head commit) to comment on.

body.description: Comment body. Use a ```suggestion block to produce a one-click suggested change.

side.description: Side of the diff. RIGHT = post-change (default), LEFT = pre-change.

start_line.description: Start line for a multi-line comment range. Omit for a single-line comment.

start_side.description: Side of the diff for start_line in a multi-line comment.

commit_id.description: Commit SHA the review is anchored to. Only used when 'comments' is non-empty. Defaults to the PR's current head SHA (auto-fetched).

repo.description: GitHub repository in 'owner/repo' format. Defaults to the repo configured in .mcp-gh-tooling.json.

suppress_errors.description: Discard stderr output. When true, gh errors are silenced.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails (non-zero exit).

tools.name: pr_comment
tools.description: Add a general comment to a pull request.

repo.description: GitHub repository in 'owner/repo' format. Defaults to the repo configured in .mcp-gh-tooling.json.

suppress_errors.description: Discard stderr output. When true, gh errors are silenced.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails (non-zero exit).

tools.name: label_add
tools.description: Add labels to a pull request or issue by name.

type.description: Whether this is a PR or issue.

repo.description: GitHub repository in 'owner/repo' format.

tools.name: label_remove
tools.description: Remove labels from a pull request or issue by name.

type.description: Whether this is a PR or issue.

repo.description: GitHub repository in 'owner/repo' format.

tools.name: assignee_add
tools.description: Assign users to a pull request or issue.

type.description: Whether this is a PR or issue.

repo.description: GitHub repository in 'owner/repo' format.

tools.name: assignee_remove
tools.description: Remove assigned users from a pull request or issue.

type.description: Whether this is a PR or issue.

repo.description: GitHub repository in 'owner/repo' format.

tools.name: sub_issue_add
tools.description: Add a sub-issue to a parent issue. Both must exist in the same repository. Uses GitHub's GraphQL sub-issues API.

sub_issue_number.description: Issue number to add as sub-issue.

repo.description: GitHub repository in 'owner/repo' format.

tools.name: sub_issue_remove
tools.description: Remove a sub-issue from a parent issue. Uses GitHub's GraphQL sub-issues API.

repo.description: GitHub repository in 'owner/repo' format.

tools.name: pr_review_reply
tools.description: Reply to an existing review comment thread on a pull request. Posts a threaded reply to a prior review comment (by its comment_id), continuing the same conversation thread. Use pr_review_submit to start a new review with inline comments; use this tool to respond to someone else's existing thread.

comment_id.description: ID of the parent review comment to reply to. Obtain from pr_comments or pr_reviews.

repo.description: GitHub repository in 'owner/repo' format. Defaults to the repo configured in .mcp-gh-tooling.json.

suppress_errors.description: Discard stderr output. When true, gh errors are silenced.

fallback.description: Text to return if the gh command fails (non-zero exit).

tools.name: project_item_add
tools.description: Add an issue or PR to a GitHub Project by project name. The server resolves the project name to its ID.

type.description: Whether this is a PR or issue.

project.description: Project name (human-readable). Server resolves to project number.

repo.description: GitHub repository in 'owner/repo' format.

tools.name: project_status_set
tools.description: Set the Status field of an issue or PR in a GitHub Project. Both project and status are specified by name — the server resolves to IDs. The item must already be in the project (use project_item_add first).

type.description: Whether this is a PR or issue.

project.description: Project name. Server resolves to project number.

status.description: Status value name (e.g., 'In Progress', 'Done'). Server resolves to option ID.

repo.description: GitHub repository in 'owner/repo' format.
